title: ISerializable should be implemented correctly
description: The ISerializable interface is the mechanism to control the type serialization process. If not implemented correctly this could result in an invalid serialization and hard-to-detect bugs. This rule raises an issue on types that implement ISerializable without following the serialization pattern recommended by Microsoft.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects classes implementing ISerializable without proper serialization constructor
logic.triggering_conditions: (Class implements ISerializable) AND (Missing protected/private serialization constructor OR Constructor is private in non-sealed class)
exclusion_conditions: Classes with proper protected constructor (SerializationInfo, StreamingContext)
never_trigger_on: Sealed classes with private serialization constructor
logic.reasoning: ISerializable requires a special constructor with signature (SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context). This constructor must be protected (to allow derived classes to deserialize) or private (if class is sealed). Missing or incorrectly scoped constructors cause runtime serialization failures.

should_trigger: [Serializable] public class Foo : ISerializable { public void GetObjectData(...) { } }
[Serializable] public class Foo : ISerializable { private Foo(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext ctx) { } }
should_not_trigger: [Serializable] public class Foo : ISerializable { protected Foo(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext ctx) { } }
[Serializable] public sealed class Bar : ISerializable { private Bar(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext ctx) { } }
